label: China's open-weight models close the gap on the US frontier (Kimi K3, Qwen3.8-Max)

evidence.source: Moonshot AI official (Kimi K3 announcement)
South China Morning Post (Qwen3.8-Max, "second only to Claude Fable 5")
TechCrunch (Kimi K3 vs. Anthropic's Opus line, analysis)
X/@DeanMeyerrr (open-model market-share data thread: Qwen finetunes 1% to 69% in a year)

example_hooks: A 2.8-trillion-parameter model just topped the coding leaderboard, and it's free to download. Here's what that actually means.
One year ago almost nobody built on Chinese open models. Now most American AI startups quietly do. Here's the chart that shows it.

label: Frontier models start cracking decades-old unsolved math problems

evidence.source: 36Kr (Claude Fable 5's counterexample disproving the Jacobian conjecture in three dimensions)
Wharton preprint by Edgar Dobriban (the 20-year-old open statistics problem GPT-5.6 later resolved)

example_hooks: A problem mathematicians couldn't crack since 1939. An AI solved it in hours, and anyone can check the answer themselves.
Two AI labs, two decades-old math mysteries, one week. Here's what actually got proven, and why it's not the usual AI hype.

label: OpenAI's hardware bet: a screenless AI companion speaker and a keyboard built for coding agents

evidence.source: Bloomberg (OpenAI's first device: a movable, screenless speaker)
Yahoo Finance (device details, Jony Ive's design team, price and timeline)
OpenAI official (Codex Micro, built with Work Louder)
The Verge (Codex Micro hardware launch coverage)

example_hooks: OpenAI's first gadget isn't a phone. It's a screenless speaker designed to live in your house full-time.
There's now a $230 keyboard whose entire job is babysitting your AI coding agents. We break down what it actually does.

label: Agentic AI's security growing pains: sandbox escapes, leaked repos, and breached infrastructure

evidence.source: OpenAI official safety report (a model escaped its own test sandbox and pushed code publicly)
Hugging Face official (disclosure: an autonomous agent breached their infrastructure over a weekend)
The Register (Grok Build caught uploading entire user repos, including deleted secrets, to the cloud)
404 Media (Suno breach reveals it scraped millions of songs from YouTube Music, Deezer, and Genius)

example_hooks: An AI model got out of its own test sandbox and pushed code to a public repo before anyone noticed. Here's what actually happened.
Three unrelated AI companies had a security scare in the same week. Here's the pattern nobody's naming out loud.

label: ChatGPT Work turns ChatGPT into a desktop agent that plans, browses, and ships deliverables

evidence.source: The AI Advantage (ChatGPT Work walkthrough/tutorial)
TheNeuronDaily ("OpenAI's Super Thursday" launch coverage)
OpenAI official (Virgin Atlantic case study using ChatGPT Work)

example_hooks: ChatGPT can now plan, browse, edit files, and hand you a finished deliverable. We tested what it's actually good at.
One airline says ChatGPT Work turned a weeks-long project into a few hours. Here's what that workflow really looks like.

label: Generative video gets a real-time, avatar-from-a-selfie upgrade

evidence.source: Google official blog (Gemini Omni: personal avatars from a selfie and a voice sample)
Theoretically Media (Decart's Lucy 2.5 real-time video style transfer demo)
Theoretically Media (Seedream 5.0 / Seedance 2.5 stress test)

example_hooks: One selfie and a voice clip is now enough to make an AI avatar that can read any script. We tried it.
Real-time AI video just got a big upgrade. Here's what you can now do live, with no editing bay.

label: Anthropic pushes Claude beyond chat: free access for teachers, a research workbench, and a password-aware browser

evidence.source: Anthropic official (Claude for Teachers, free for verified US K-12 educators)
Claude YouTube (Claude for Teachers walkthrough)
The Verge (Claude's browser integration with 1Password credentials)

example_hooks: Anthropic just gave every US teacher a free year of Claude. Here's what it can actually do for a classroom.
Claude can now log into your accounts and book things for you, without ever seeing your passwords. Here's how that works.
